title: Cost of Capital: If a company has a required return of 12%, a...
summary: Calculate or evaluate the cost of capital using appropriate financial models.
prompt: If a company has a required return of 12%, a growth rate of 3%, and a current dividend of $4, what is the value of the stock using the Gordon Growth Model?
solution: Value = D1 / (k - g) = $4 × (1 + 0.03) / (0.12 - 0.03) = $4.12 / 0.09 = $45.78. This model assumes constant growth, and the calculated value provides insight into the stock's worth based on future dividend payments.
